Package org.areslib.hardware.faults

Class RobotHealthTracker

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.command.SubsystemBase
org.areslib.hardware.faults.RobotHealthTracker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Subsystem
public class RobotHealthTracker extends SubsystemBase
Global singleton subsystem that continuously checks for hardware faults across all registered FaultMonitor devices and blasts them to both AdvantageScope telemetry AND the AresFaultManager alert system (which provides gamepad rumble and LED feedback).

Must be registered with the CommandScheduler for its periodic() to run. This is handled automatically by AresCommandOpMode.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static RobotHealthTracker getInstance()
      Returns the singleton instance (thread-safe).
      Returns:
      The global RobotHealthTracker.

    • reset

      public static void reset()
      Resets the singleton. Must be called during OpMode transitions to prevent stale monitors from previous OpModes.

    • registerMonitor

      public void registerMonitor(FaultMonitor monitor)
      Registers a new fault monitor to be checked every robot loop.
      Parameters:
      monitor - The hardware component implementing FaultMonitor.

    • hasActiveFaults

      public boolean hasActiveFaults()
      Returns true if any registered monitor currently reports a hardware fault.
      Returns:
      True if at least one fault is active.

    • periodic

      public void periodic()
      Description copied from interface: Subsystem
      This method is called periodically by the CommandScheduler. Useful for updating subsystem-specific state that you don't want to defer to a Command.
